instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `check-same-length` that evaluates two sequence expressions exactly once and signals an error if their LENGTH values differ.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro check-same-length (seq1 seq2)
  (let ((g1 (gensym)) (g2 (gensym)))
    `(let ((,g1 ,seq1) (,g2 ,seq2))
       (unless (= (length ,g1) (length ,g2))
         (error "Sequences have different lengths: ~A vs ~A" (length ,g1) (length ,g2))))))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 KEYS) (#:G2 VALUES))
            (UNLESS (= (LENGTH #:G1) (LENGTH #:G2))
              (ERROR "Sequences have different lengths: ~A vs ~A" (LENGTH #:G1)
                     (LENGTH #:G2))))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 KEYS) (#:G113 VALUES))
            (UNLESS (= (LENGTH #:G112) (LENGTH #:G113))
              (ERROR "Sequences have different lengths: ~A vs ~A"
                     (LENGTH #:G112) (LENGTH #:G113))))
